title: Earthy Greek Lamb Tagine
description: A robust, fermented lamb tagine with Mediterranean herbs, perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: In a bowl, combine lamb mince with fermented Greek yogurt, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper and mix until thoroughly coated.
Let the mixture marinate for 30 minutes at room temperature or cover and refrigerate for up to 2 hours.
Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat and sauté onion until translucent.
Add carrots and bell pepper to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the marinated lamb mixture and cook until the lamb changes color, about 8 minutes.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and reduce the heat to low, then cover the pot.
Simmer the tagine for 45 minutes, stirring gently every 15 minutes, until the lamb is tender and the sauce thickens.
Stir in fresh oregano leaves and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tagine rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with lemon zest and serve hot with a side of cauliflower rice or crusty bread.
